consistency.level_school_match: Consistent. Level 5 Necromancy is standard for this effect.
consistency.casting_time_duration_logic: Consistent. A 1-hour casting time for returning a creature to life is reasonable, resulting in an instantaneous effect upon completion.
consistency.components_vs_effect: Consistent. High-cost material component (500gp diamond consumption) aligns with a powerful resurrection effect.

quality.clarity: High. The description clearly outlines preconditions (death duration, soul availability), immediate results (1 HP, poison/disease removal), limitations (no missing parts, undead exceptions), and lingering penalties.
quality.completeness: Complete for a standard 5th-level spell description, covering resurrection conditions and consequences.
quality.flavor: Appropriate for a high-stakes necromantic spell that treads the line between life and death.

mechanics.trigger: Touch upon a creature dead no longer than 10 days.
success_conditions: Target soul must be at liberty to rejoin the body.
Body must be intact (no missing integral parts like the head).
positive_outcomes: Target returns to life with 1 hit point.
Cures nonmagical poisons active at the time of death.
negative_outcomes_and_limitations: Magical diseases, curses, etc., take effect upon revival if not already removed.
Cannot return undead creatures to life.
Does not restore missing body parts.
Imposes a cumulative penalty: -4 to all rolls (attacks, saves, checks) immediately upon resurrection.
mechanics.progression: The -4 penalty is reduced by 1 after every long rest until it disappears (meaning 4 long rests remove it entirely).
